reasoning: The 6-month T-bill rate reflects market expectations of the Fed Funds path. A rate of 3.69% suggests that the market is currently pricing in a cutting cycle. Over a roughly 3-month period (from late October to January), the rate could move in either direction based on upcoming macroeconomic data, but the overarching expected path of policy rates is downwards, which places a slight structural headwind against pure increases in yields, absent upside inflation surprises. Given the generally anticipated easing path, I estimate a slightly lower-than-even probability (around 40%) that the rate will increase from its October anchor by the resolution date.
